title: ombs_senegal
text: This file will become your README and also the index of your documentation.

title: ombs_senegal
text:
Developer Guide
If you are new to using nbdev here are some useful pointers to get you started.

Install ombs_senegal in Development mode
# make sure ombs_senegal package is installed in development mode
$ pip install -e .

# make changes under nbs/ directory
# ...

# compile to have changes apply to ombs_senegal
$ nbdev_prepare

title: ombs_senegal
text:
Usage

Installation
Install latest from the GitHub repository:
$ pip install git+https://github.com/iraind/ombs_senegal.git
or from conda
$ conda install -c iraind ombs_senegal
or from pypi
$ pip install ombs_senegal


Documentation
Documentation can be found hosted on this GitHub repository’s pages. Additionally you can find package manager specific guidelines on conda and pypi respectively.

title: Benchmark Models
text:
Scoring
Since we have generated multiple predictions with different parameters, we will select only the best performing models according to each metric

source

BenchmarkScores

 BenchmarkScores ()

*A class to compute and compare different error metrics between predictions and observations.
This class provides a flexible way to compute multiple error metrics between predicted and observed values. New metrics can be easily added by implementing them as methods. The new metrics can then be used by passing their method names as strings to compute_scores().
Example:
```python class CustomBenchmarkScores(BenchmarkScores): def mse(self, pred, obs): error = ((pred - obs)**2).mean(dim=“time”) error.name = “mse” return error

title: Benchmark Models
text:
Save prediction data
We save the best model for later use

title: Benchmark Models
text:
Time series verification
Now that we have identified the optimal model parameters, let’s verify its performance by comparing the predicted discharge values with both observed values and MGB model predictions. This comparison will be done across the full 10-day prediction horizon to assess how well our model maintains its predictive power over time. We’ll visualize these comparisons using time series plots that show the observed discharge, our model’s predictions, and the MGB model predictions side by side.

source

plot_prediction_comparison

 plot_prediction_comparison (observed:xarray.core.dataarray.DataArray,
                             predicted:xarray.core.dataset.Dataset,
                             mgb:xarray.core.dataarray.DataArray)

Plot comparison between observed, predicted and MGB discharge values for a 10-day horizon.









Type
Details




observed
DataArray
Time series of observed water discharge values


predicted
Dataset
Dataset containing predicted discharge values for each forecast horizon (t+1 to t+10)


mgb
DataArray
Time series of MGB model water discharge predictions


Returns
Figure
